title: Senior Battery Systems Engineer
description: VoltDrive Motors is seeking a Senior Battery Systems Engineer to lead the design and development of next-generation lithium-ion battery packs for our flagship EV sedan platform. You will own the full lifecycle from concept through production launch, working closely with our thermal management and vehicle integration teams. This role demands deep expertise in cell-to-pack architecture, high-voltage safety interlocks, and Battery Management Systems (BMS) tuning. You will develop detailed 3D models in SOLIDWORKS and perform structural analysis using ANSYS to validate pack enclosure designs under crash and vibration loading. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced environment where cross-functional collaboration is the norm. You will interface with suppliers for cell procurement, author DFMEA documents, and support prototype builds at our Detroit pilot facility. We value engineers who bring both analytical rigor and creative problem-solving to the table. Our team is growing rapidly as we prepare for SOP in Q3 2027, and this is an opportunity to shape the future of American EV manufacturing from the ground up.
requirements:
- BS or MS in Mechanical Engineering or related field
- 7+ years of experience in battery pack design for electric vehicles
- Expert-level proficiency in SOLIDWORKS; CSWP certification preferred
- Strong understanding of GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- Experience with Finite Element Analysis (FEA) using ANSYS or similar tools
- Working knowledge of BMS hardware and software integration
- Familiarity with DFMEA and APQP processes
- Experience with thermal analysis of battery systems
- Excellent technical writing and documentation skills
- Ability to travel domestically up to 15%

title: EV Powertrain Design Engineer
description: Apex EV Systems is a venture-backed startup building the next wave of high-performance electric powertrains for commercial fleet vehicles. We are looking for a Powertrain Design Engineer to join our small but mighty engineering team in Palo Alto. You will design electric powertrain components including motor housings, gearbox assemblies, and driveshaft interfaces. Our stack is SOLIDWORKS for CAD, MATLAB/Simulink for system modeling, and Python scripts for data post-processing. You will perform tolerance stack-up analysis to ensure robust assemblies and apply DFM/DFA principles to keep our bill of materials lean. This is a hands-on role where you will spend time on the shop floor working alongside technicians during prototype builds. We move fast, ship often, and believe great engineering comes from tight feedback loops between design and test. If you are tired of big-company bureaucracy and want to see your work on the road within months instead of years, this is your gig. Competitive salary, meaningful equity, and unlimited PTO.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering; MS preferred
- 3-5 years of experience in electric powertrain or drivetrain design
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS and AutoCAD
- Strong grasp of GD&T and tolerance stack-up analysis
- Experience with MATLAB and Simulink for system-level modeling
- Knowledge of DFM/DFA and design for manufacturing best practices
- Familiarity with FEA tools for structural analysis
- Python scripting ability for data analysis and automation
- Understanding of CAN bus communication protocols
- Self-starter mentality comfortable in a startup environment

title: Thermal Management Engineer
description: ChargePoint Engineering is hiring a Thermal Management Engineer to design and optimize cooling systems for our next-generation EV battery packs and power electronics. In this role, you will develop thermal models using ANSYS Fluent and conduct CFD simulations to evaluate coolant flow distribution, heat exchanger performance, and cold plate designs. You will also perform thermal analysis on inverter and onboard charger assemblies to ensure components remain within safe operating temperatures across all drive cycles. Collaboration is central to this position. You will work with battery engineers on BMS thermal integration, with the vehicle team on HVAC load balancing, and with manufacturing on DFM considerations for brazed aluminum cold plates. The ideal candidate has a strong foundation in heat transfer fundamentals and enjoys bridging the gap between simulation and physical testing. Our Fremont lab has a full suite of thermal chambers and dynamometers, so you will validate your models against real-world data regularly. We are a growing team with a culture that rewards intellectual curiosity and technical depth.
requirements:
- MS in Mechanical Engineering with focus on thermal/fluids
- 4+ years of thermal management experience in automotive or EV applications
- Proficiency in ANSYS Fluent or similar CFD tools
- Experience with thermal analysis of battery packs and power electronics
- Strong CAD skills in SOLIDWORKS or CATIA
- Understanding of GD&T and tolerance analysis
- Familiarity with Battery Management Systems and thermal runaway mitigation
- Knowledge of FEA methods for thermo-mechanical stress analysis
- MATLAB proficiency for data processing and model correlation
- Experience with DFM for thermal system components

title: Chassis Design Engineer
description: Meridian Auto Group, a Tier 1 supplier to major OEMs, is looking for a Chassis Design Engineer to support the design and release of suspension and subframe components for electric vehicle platforms. You will create detailed part and assembly models in CATIA V5, generate production-intent drawings with proper GD&T callouts, and work with our FEA team to ensure structural integrity under static and dynamic load cases. This role is heavily focused on sheet metal and stamped component design, so experience with forming simulation and DFM for stamping processes is essential. You will also participate in DFMEA reviews and support APQP deliverables for program milestones. Our Dearborn office sits at the heart of the American auto industry, and you will collaborate daily with OEM customer engineering teams. We offer a structured career development path, tuition reimbursement, and a hybrid work schedule. If you want to work on EV platforms that will be on roads across North America, Meridian is the place to grow your career.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering
- 5+ years of chassis or body structure design experience
- Expert proficiency in CATIA V5/V6
- Strong knowledge of GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- Experience with sheet metal design and stamping processes
- Understanding of FEA for structural and fatigue analysis
- Familiarity with DFMEA, APQP, and PPAP processes
- Knowledge of IATF 16949 quality management standards
- AutoCAD experience for legacy drawing support
- Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills

title: Battery Pack Mechanical Engineer
description: NovaBattery Corp designs and manufactures battery modules and packs for the EV industry. We are looking for a Battery Pack Mechanical Engineer to join our Austin team. You will be responsible for the mechanical design of battery enclosures, module frames, busbar assemblies, and thermal interface materials. Your day-to-day will involve 3D modeling in SOLIDWORKS, running FEA simulations for vibration and shock load cases, and working with our testing lab to correlate simulation results with physical test data. You should have solid experience with GD&T, tolerance stack-up analysis, and design for manufacturing. Knowledge of Battery Management Systems is a plus since you will interface closely with our electrical engineering team on sensor placement and harness routing. We are a mid-size company with the agility of a startup and the stability of established OEM contracts. Our Austin facility features state-of-the-art prototyping capabilities including 3D printing for rapid iteration on bracket and housing concepts. If you are passionate about energy storage and want to work with a team that ships real products, we want to hear from you.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering or equivalent
- 3-7 years of battery pack or module mechanical design experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS; Fusion 360 experience a bonus
- Strong understanding of GD&T and tolerance stack-up
- FEA experience (ANSYS or SolidWorks Simulation)
- Knowledge of DFM/DFA principles
- Familiarity with BMS integration and high-voltage safety
- Experience with 3D printing and additive manufacturing for prototyping
- Understanding of thermal analysis for battery systems
- MATLAB or Python skills for data analysis

title: EV Motor Design Engineer
description: TorqueWave Technologies is pushing the boundaries of electric motor design for high-performance EV applications. We need a Motor Design Engineer who can take concepts from electromagnetic simulation through detailed mechanical design to production release. You will work on interior permanent magnet (IPM) and switched reluctance motor topologies, designing rotor and stator assemblies, bearing systems, and motor housings. SOLIDWORKS is our primary CAD platform, but you will also use MATLAB and Simulink for motor performance modeling and control algorithm validation. Thermal analysis is critical in this role since our motors operate at extreme power densities, and you will use both analytical methods and ANSYS to predict temperature distributions under continuous and peak torque conditions. We value lightweight optimization and expect you to apply topology optimization techniques to reduce mass without sacrificing structural integrity. Our San Jose office has a collaborative open-floor layout, daily standups, and a culture that blends rigorous engineering with Silicon Valley speed. Join us and help define what the next generation of EV propulsion looks like.
requirements:
- MS or PhD in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering
- 5+ years of electric motor design experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS and AutoCAD
- Strong background in thermal analysis and FEA
- Experience with MATLAB/Simulink for motor modeling
- Knowledge of GD&T for precision rotating assemblies
- Understanding of DFM for lamination stacking and winding processes
- Familiarity with vibration analysis and NVH considerations
- Experience with lightweight optimization and topology optimization
- Python scripting skills for simulation automation

title: Vehicle Integration Engineer
description: ElectraFleet Inc. is electrifying last-mile delivery. Our Nashville engineering center is hiring a Vehicle Integration Engineer to coordinate the mechanical integration of battery, powertrain, chassis, and body systems into our Class 3-5 electric delivery trucks. This is a cross-functional role where you will manage interface definitions, resolve packaging conflicts, and ensure all subsystems work together harmoniously. You will use SOLIDWORKS and AutoCAD for packaging studies and maintain our master vehicle BOM. Strong project management skills are a must since you will track integration milestones across multiple programs simultaneously. Understanding of CAN bus architecture is important as you will work with the controls team on network topology and signal routing. You will also support vehicle-level DFMEA activities and participate in design reviews with our manufacturing plant in Chattanooga. This is not a desk-only job. You will spend time at our prototype shop and test track, so bring your steel-toed boots and a willingness to get hands-on. We offer competitive compensation, relocation assistance, and the chance to decarbonize freight transportation.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical or Automotive Engineering
- 4-8 years of vehicle integration or systems engineering experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS and AutoCAD
- Understanding of GD&T and tolerance management
- Experience with CAN bus and vehicle communication networks
- Knowledge of DFMEA and systems engineering (MBSE) principles
- Project management experience; PMP or Agile certification a plus
- Familiarity with electric powertrain components and BMS
- Strong technical writing and documentation skills
- Willingness to travel between Nashville and Chattanooga facilities

title: Suspension Engineer
description: PinnacleDrive Automotive is developing a clean-sheet EV skateboard platform and we need a talented Suspension Engineer to design our front and rear suspension systems. You will be responsible for kinematic and compliance (K&C) analysis, component design, and hardware specification for control arms, knuckles, springs, dampers, and stabilizer bars. We use CATIA V5 for CAD and ADAMS for multi-body dynamics simulation. Structural analysis using FEA is a core part of the role, and you will work closely with our durability team to ensure components meet fatigue life targets under customer usage profiles. Strong knowledge of GD&T is required for creating production-intent drawings that our suppliers can quote and manufacture. You should also be comfortable with DFM considerations for forged and cast aluminum suspension components. Familiarity with regenerative braking system integration is a bonus since suspension tuning must account for regen torque effects on vehicle dynamics. We are based in Detroit and offer a hybrid work model with three days per week on-site.
requirements:
- BS or MS in Mechanical Engineering
- 5+ years of suspension design experience in automotive
- Proficiency in CATIA V5 or SOLIDWORKS
- Experience with FEA for structural and fatigue analysis
- Strong GD&T knowledge per ASME Y14.5
- Understanding of vehicle dynamics and K&C analysis
- Familiarity with ADAMS or similar MBD tools
- Knowledge of DFM for casting and forging processes
- Experience with vibration analysis and ride comfort tuning
- Understanding of regenerative braking and its effect on suspension loads

title: Body-in-White Engineer
description: SteelArc Manufacturing is a leading body structure supplier seeking a Body-in-White (BIW) Engineer for our Toledo facility. You will design and optimize structural body components including A/B/C pillars, rocker panels, floor pans, and cross members for EV platforms. Weight reduction is a critical focus area, and you will apply lightweight optimization strategies using advanced high-strength steels and multi-material joining techniques. Your toolbox will include SOLIDWORKS for design, AutoCAD for plant layouts, and FEA tools for crash and stiffness analysis. This role requires deep knowledge of sheet metal forming, including stamping, roll forming, and hydroforming processes. You will collaborate with die engineering on DFM feasibility studies and support launch activities on the shop floor. IATF 16949 and ISO 9001 quality systems knowledge is expected, as is experience with APQP deliverables. Toledo has a rich automotive heritage, and SteelArc has been part of it for over 40 years. We offer excellent benefits including a pension plan and on-site fitness center.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering
- 6+ years of BIW or body structure design experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS and AutoCAD
- Strong FEA skills for crash and stiffness analysis
- Expert knowledge of GD&T
- Extensive experience with sheet metal design and stamping processes
- Understanding of IATF 16949 and ISO 9001 quality systems
- Familiarity with APQP and PPAP processes
- Knowledge of lightweight optimization and advanced materials
- Lean manufacturing experience (kaizen, 5S) preferred

title: NVH Engineer
description: QuietRide EV Labs specializes in noise, vibration, and harshness engineering for premium electric vehicles. We are hiring an NVH Engineer to support our growing portfolio of OEM consulting projects. EVs present unique NVH challenges since the absence of engine masking noise makes other sources like gear whine, road noise, and HVAC more perceptible. You will conduct vibration analysis on body structures, powertrain mounts, and suspension components using a combination of experimental modal analysis and FEA simulation. Your work in SOLIDWORKS will focus on designing isolation mounts, acoustic barriers, and structural reinforcements. You will use MATLAB for signal processing and transfer path analysis, and Python for automating data acquisition workflows. Familiarity with CAN bus data logging is helpful for correlating in-vehicle measurements with operating conditions. We are a small, specialized team that works directly with some of the biggest names in the EV space, so you will have exposure to cutting-edge platforms and technologies. Our Irvine office is a short drive from the beach, and we believe in work-life balance alongside engineering excellence.
requirements:
- MS in Mechanical Engineering with emphasis on dynamics/acoustics
- 3-6 years of NVH engineering experience, preferably in EV or automotive
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS
- Strong FEA skills for modal and frequency response analysis
- Experience with vibration analysis and experimental modal testing
- MATLAB proficiency for signal processing and data analysis
- Python programming skills
- Knowledge of GD&T
- Familiarity with CAN bus data acquisition
- Understanding of thermal analysis effects on NVH (thermal expansion impacts)

title: Test Validation Engineer
description: ProveTech Automotive is hiring a Test Validation Engineer to plan and execute mechanical testing for EV components and subsystems. You will own the validation of battery enclosures, powertrain mounts, suspension components, and structural brackets from early development through production sign-off. This means designing test fixtures in SOLIDWORKS, writing test procedures, analyzing results in MATLAB, and presenting findings to program teams. You will work with environmental chambers, servo-hydraulic test frames, vibration shakers, and our in-house crash sled. Experience with fatigue analysis is important because you will design accelerated durability tests that replicate years of customer usage in weeks. You should understand GD&T well enough to define critical measurement points for CMM inspection and gauge R&R studies. Statistical process control (SPC) knowledge is valuable for analyzing production part variability. We are a test-focused organization that prides itself on data-driven engineering decisions. Our Ann Arbor facility is expanding, and this role offers clear growth opportunities into test management.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering
- 3-5 years of mechanical testing and validation experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS for fixture design
- MATLAB skills for data analysis and reporting
- Understanding of GD&T and CMM inspection
- Experience with fatigue analysis and accelerated life testing
- Knowledge of statistical process control (SPC)
- Familiarity with FEA for test-analysis correlation
- Understanding of BMS and high-voltage safety protocols for battery testing
- Strong technical writing skills for test reports and procedures

title: Quality Engineer - Automotive
description: Precision EV Components is a Tier 2 supplier of machined and stamped parts for EV battery and powertrain systems. We need a Quality Engineer at our Louisville plant to maintain and improve our quality management systems. You will lead root cause analysis using 8D methodology for customer complaints, conduct internal audits per IATF 16949, and manage PPAP submissions for new product launches. This role requires strong knowledge of APQP, DFMEA/PFMEA, and control plan development. You will use statistical process control (SPC) to monitor critical-to-quality dimensions and work with manufacturing to implement corrective actions. Experience reading engineering drawings with GD&T callouts is essential since you will be the quality gatekeeper for incoming and outgoing parts. AutoCAD or SOLIDWORKS skills are helpful for reviewing part models. We operate a Lean Six Sigma culture, and Green Belt certification is preferred. Our Louisville facility runs three shifts producing over 2 million parts per month, so this is a high-volume, high-impact role. We offer a competitive salary, annual bonus, and comprehensive benefits.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical or Industrial Engineering
- 4+ years of quality engineering experience in automotive manufacturing
- Strong knowledge of IATF 16949 and ISO 9001
- Experience with APQP, PPAP, and DFMEA/PFMEA
- Proficiency in root cause analysis (8D methodology)
- Understanding of GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- Statistical process control (SPC) expertise
- Lean Six Sigma Green Belt certification preferred
- Familiarity with SOLIDWORKS or AutoCAD
- Knowledge of ERP systems (SAP preferred)
- Experience with lean manufacturing principles (kaizen, 5S)

title: Manufacturing Engineer - EV Assembly
description: VoltDrive Motors is building a brand-new EV assembly plant in Chattanooga and we need Manufacturing Engineers to help bring it to life. You will design assembly processes, specify tooling and fixtures, and develop standard work instructions for our battery pack and final vehicle assembly lines. This is a ground-floor opportunity to influence factory layout, material flow, and automation strategy. SOLIDWORKS and AutoCAD will be your primary tools for workstation design and plant layout. You should have experience with DFM/DFA analysis and be able to partner with product design engineers to improve manufacturability early in the development cycle. Familiarity with lean manufacturing principles including kaizen events, 5S, and value stream mapping is expected. You will also support PFMEA development and work within our IATF 16949 quality framework. Understanding of high-voltage safety for EV assembly operations is important. We are offering relocation packages to Chattanooga, a city with an incredible outdoor lifestyle, low cost of living, and a growing tech scene. Come build something from scratch.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical, Industrial, or Manufacturing Engineering
- 3-7 years of manufacturing engineering experience, preferably in automotive
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS and AutoCAD
- Experience with DFM/DFA analysis
- Knowledge of lean manufacturing (kaizen, 5S, value stream mapping)
- Understanding of PFMEA and process control plans
- Familiarity with IATF 16949 quality standards
- Experience with ERP systems (SAP) for BOM and routing management
- GD&T knowledge for fixture and gauge design
- Understanding of high-voltage safety requirements for EV assembly

title: Charging Systems Engineer
description: GridLink EV Solutions designs and manufactures DC fast-charging equipment for the rapidly expanding EV charging network. We are looking for a Charging Systems Engineer to design mechanical enclosures, thermal management systems, and cable assemblies for our next-generation 350kW charging stations. You will model components in Fusion 360 and SOLIDWORKS, perform thermal analysis to ensure power electronics stay within operating limits, and work with our industrial design team on aesthetics and user interface ergonomics. Your designs must survive outdoor installation environments, so experience with IP67 enclosure design, corrosion protection, and environmental sealing is important. You will apply DFM principles for sheet metal fabrication and injection molding processes. Knowledge of CAN bus or Modbus communication protocols is helpful for coordinating with the controls team. We value engineers who think holistically about the product experience. Our San Jose office is dog-friendly, has an on-site gym, and offers flexible work hours. If you want to help build the infrastructure that makes EVs practical for everyone, GridLink is the place.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering
- 3-5 years of product design experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS and Fusion 360
- Experience with thermal analysis for power electronics cooling
- Understanding of GD&T and tolerance analysis
- Knowledge of DFM for sheet metal and injection molding
- Familiarity with IP-rated enclosure design
- AutoCAD skills for installation drawings
- Understanding of CAN bus or industrial communication protocols
- Experience with FEA for structural load cases

title: High Voltage Systems Engineer
description: AmpereX Automotive is developing a modular EV platform for ride-sharing and autonomous vehicle applications. We need a High Voltage Systems Engineer to design and validate the high-voltage distribution architecture, including battery disconnect units, junction boxes, cabling, and connector systems. Safety is paramount in this role. You will perform DFMEA on all HV components, ensure compliance with SAE J1772 and UL standards, and support homologation testing. SOLIDWORKS is used for component and harness routing models, and you will leverage MATLAB for electrical system modeling. A strong understanding of Battery Management Systems is required since you will define the interface between the BMS and the HV distribution system. You will also work with the structural team on crash-safe routing of HV cables, performing FEA to assess connector retention under impact loads. GD&T expertise is needed for designing mating interfaces with tight tolerances. This is a safety-critical role at the heart of EV architecture, and we need someone with meticulous attention to detail and a deep respect for electrical safety standards.
requirements:
- BS or MS in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering
- 5+ years of high-voltage systems experience in EV or hybrid vehicles
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS
- Strong GD&T skills
- DFMEA experience for safety-critical components
- Knowledge of BMS integration and HV safety standards (SAE J1772, UL)
- FEA experience for connector and bracket analysis
- MATLAB proficiency for system modeling
- Understanding of CAN bus communication for HV system monitoring
- Familiarity with structural analysis under crash loading conditions

title: CAD Designer - Automotive
description: DesignWorks Studio provides contract engineering services to major EV OEMs. We are hiring a CAD Designer to create production-quality 3D models and 2D drawings for automotive interior and exterior components. You will work in SOLIDWORKS daily, translating design sketches and engineering requirements into fully detailed models ready for tooling release. GD&T is your second language, and you take pride in clean, well-organized model trees. This position requires experience with surfacing for Class A and Class B surfaces, as well as parametric modeling for structural brackets and hardware. Familiarity with Fusion 360 and AutoCAD is a plus for cross-platform projects. You will support multiple clients simultaneously, so time management and clear communication are essential. Our Irvine office has a relaxed creative atmosphere with regular design critiques and lunch-and-learns. We are looking for someone who geeks out over elegant geometry and perfect drawings. CSWP certification is strongly preferred.
requirements:
- Associate's or Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Design or related field
- 4+ years of automotive CAD design experience
- Expert proficiency in SOLIDWORKS; CSWP certification strongly preferred
- Thorough knowledge of GD&T per ASME Y14.5
- Experience with surfacing and complex geometry modeling
- Familiarity with Fusion 360 and AutoCAD
- Understanding of DFM for injection molding and stamping
- Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously
- Strong attention to detail in drawing creation
- Knowledge of tolerance stack-up analysis

title: R&D Engineer - Electric Vehicles
description: Frontier EV Research is an advanced R&D lab focused on next-generation EV technologies. We are hiring an R&D Engineer to work on exploratory projects spanning battery chemistry integration, novel motor topologies, and advanced thermal management concepts. This is a highly technical role where you will split your time between concept design in SOLIDWORKS, simulation in ANSYS and MATLAB, and hands-on lab work with prototype hardware. We encourage our engineers to publish papers and file patents, so strong technical writing skills are essential. You will leverage Python and machine learning techniques for design space exploration and multi-objective optimization. CFD simulation experience is valuable for evaluating new cooling architectures. A background in Finite Element Analysis is expected for structural validation of novel concepts. You will also explore additive manufacturing techniques for rapid prototyping of complex geometries that cannot be produced with traditional methods. Our Austin lab is equipped with a thermal imaging suite, battery cycling stations, and a materials characterization lab. If you are driven by curiosity and want to work at the frontier of EV technology, apply now.
requirements:
- MS or PhD in Mechanical Engineering
- 2-5 years of R&D experience in EV or energy storage
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS and ANSYS
- Strong MATLAB and Python programming skills
- Experience with Finite Element Analysis
- Knowledge of CFD for thermal/fluid systems
- Familiarity with machine learning for engineering optimization
- Understanding of GD&T
- Experience with 3D printing and additive manufacturing
- Strong technical writing and publication skills
- Thermal analysis expertise for battery and power electronics

title: Durability Engineer
description: EnduroTest Automotive is a testing and validation company serving the EV industry. We are seeking a Durability Engineer to develop and execute fatigue life prediction methodologies for EV structural and powertrain components. You will use FEA-based fatigue analysis tools including nCode DesignLife and ANSYS to predict component life under complex multi-axial loading. Your work starts with customer usage data and ends with a validated fatigue life prediction that gives our clients confidence in their designs. SOLIDWORKS proficiency is required for building FEA-ready geometry, and MATLAB is used extensively for load data processing, rainflow counting, and damage accumulation calculations. GD&T expertise helps you identify critical features and potential failure initiation sites. You will write detailed technical reports and present findings to client engineering teams. Understanding of structural analysis principles is fundamental, and experience with vibration analysis for resonance avoidance is a plus. We also support clients with root cause analysis when field failures occur, so experience with 8D methodology is valuable. Detroit is the epicenter of automotive engineering, and EnduroTest gives you exposure to multiple OEM programs simultaneously.
requirements:
- MS in Mechanical Engineering
- 4-7 years of durability or fatigue engineering experience
- Experience with fatigue analysis tools (nCode, Fe-Safe, or similar)
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS and ANSYS
- Strong FEA skills for structural analysis
- MATLAB proficiency for load data analysis
- Understanding of GD&T
- Experience with vibration analysis
- Knowledge of root cause analysis and 8D methodology
- Strong technical writing and presentation skills
- Familiarity with automotive durability test standards

title: Crash Safety Engineer
description: SafeVolt Dynamics is dedicated to making electric vehicles the safest on the road. We are hiring a Crash Safety Engineer to develop and validate crashworthiness designs for EV body structures and battery enclosures. You will use LS-DYNA and ANSYS for full-vehicle and component-level crash simulations, interpreting results to guide structural improvements that meet or exceed FMVSS and Euro NCAP requirements. CATIA is our primary CAD tool for body structure design, and you will work closely with the BIW team on crush zone optimization and load path management. Lightweight optimization is central to this role since every kilogram saved from the body structure extends range, but safety cannot be compromised. You will perform structural analysis on joints, welds, and adhesive bonds to validate their performance under impact loading. Experience with sheet metal design and advanced high-strength steels is highly valued. We operate within an IATF 16949 quality system and expect our engineers to contribute to DFMEA activities. Strong GD&T knowledge is required for tolerance-sensitive crash structure assemblies.
requirements:
- BS or MS in Mechanical Engineering
- 5+ years of automotive crash safety experience
- Proficiency in CATIA V5 and LS-DYNA
- FEA expertise for crash simulation and structural analysis
- Strong GD&T knowledge
- Experience with lightweight optimization and advanced materials
- Knowledge of FMVSS and Euro NCAP requirements
- Understanding of sheet metal design and forming processes
- DFMEA experience
- Familiarity with IATF 16949 quality standards
- MATLAB or Python for post-processing simulation results

title: Prototype Build Engineer
description: RapidEV Labs is a prototype development shop that builds concept and pre-production EVs for OEMs and startups. We need a Prototype Build Engineer who can take designs from screen to steel. You will manage the build process for prototype vehicles, coordinating with machinists, welders, and electricians to assemble one-off and small-batch vehicles. This role requires reading complex engineering drawings with GD&T, troubleshooting fit and assembly issues in real-time, and feeding back design improvements to the engineering team. SOLIDWORKS is our CAD tool, and you will create fixture designs, modification packages, and build documentation. Experience with CNC machining processes is important since you will spec custom parts and work with our machine shop. Knowledge of 3D printing is valuable for quick-turn brackets and adapters. You should understand BMS and high-voltage safety for battery pack integration during builds. This is a shop-floor role with some office time, and you need to be comfortable working with your hands. Our Austin shop has a great crew and a culture of getting things done right. If you love building things, this is your dream job.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering or equivalent hands-on experience
- 3-5 years of prototype build or vehicle assembly experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS
- Strong GD&T reading and interpretation skills
- Experience with CNC machining and fabrication processes
- Familiarity with 3D printing for rapid prototyping
- Understanding of BMS and high-voltage safety
- Knowledge of DFM/DFA principles
- Technical writing skills for build procedures
- Hands-on mechanical aptitude and problem-solving ability

title: Senior Thermal Systems Architect
description: VoltDrive Motors is seeking a Senior Thermal Systems Architect to define and lead the thermal management strategy across our entire vehicle platform. This executive-level individual contributor role requires someone who can architect integrated thermal systems spanning battery cooling, cabin climate, motor cooling, and power electronics thermal management. You will build system-level models in MATLAB/Simulink, conduct CFD analysis using ANSYS Fluent, and guide a team of thermal engineers in detailed component design. Decision-making will be informed by thermal analysis and validated with extensive test data from our prototype fleet. This role demands experience with both air-cooled and liquid-cooled systems, heat pump integration, and waste heat recovery strategies for range extension. You will interface with the BMS team on thermal protection algorithms and with the vehicle controls team on energy management optimization. SOLIDWORKS proficiency is expected for reviewing component designs. The ideal candidate has 10+ years of experience and a track record of shipping thermal systems in production vehicles. We offer a senior-level compensation package including RSUs and annual performance bonuses.
requirements:
- MS or PhD in Mechanical Engineering (thermal/fluids focus)
- 10+ years of thermal systems experience in automotive or EV
- Expert in MATLAB/Simulink for system modeling
- Proficiency in CFD tools (ANSYS Fluent or Star-CCM+)
- Strong thermal analysis background
- Experience with SOLIDWORKS or CATIA
- Knowledge of GD&T
- Understanding of BMS thermal integration
- Experience with FEA for thermo-structural analysis
- Familiarity with Python for scripting and automation
- Strong technical writing and leadership skills

title: EV Powertrain Test Engineer
description: DynaPower Systems develops electric powertrain solutions for commercial vehicles. We are hiring an EV Powertrain Test Engineer to plan, execute, and analyze tests on electric motors, inverters, and gearbox assemblies at our Greenville test facility. You will commission and maintain dynamometer test cells, develop test procedures aligned with industry standards, and process results using MATLAB and Python. Understanding of electric powertrain architecture including motor, inverter, gearbox, and half-shaft interfaces is essential. You will use SOLIDWORKS to design custom test fixtures and adapters, applying GD&T to ensure repeatable test setups. The role involves significant data analysis work, and we are beginning to explore machine learning approaches for anomaly detection in test data. Familiarity with CAN bus data logging and vehicle communication protocols is important for system-level testing. You will also perform vibration analysis on powertrain assemblies to identify resonance issues and gear mesh frequencies. Our Greenville facility is expanding with new test cells for next-generation 800V powertrains. This is a hands-on role with growth opportunities into test management.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering
- 3-6 years of powertrain testing experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS
- Strong MATLAB and Python skills for data analysis
- Understanding of GD&T for test fixture design
- Experience with CAN bus data acquisition
- Knowledge of vibration analysis and frequency analysis
- Familiarity with electric powertrain components
- Understanding of FEA for fixture structural validation
- Experience with machine learning techniques is a plus
- Strong technical writing skills for test reports

title: Mechanical Design Engineer - Interiors
description: ComfortTech EV designs premium interior systems for electric vehicles. We are looking for a Mechanical Design Engineer to develop seat structures, dashboard assemblies, center consoles, and trim components. Our design philosophy centers on creating features that delight customers while maintaining rigorous engineering standards. You will work primarily in SOLIDWORKS with supplementary work in Fusion 360 for concept exploration. Every design must account for DFM considerations including injection molding, thermoforming, and upholstery processes. GD&T is critical for maintaining fit and finish across complex interior assemblies with tight gap and flush specifications. You will conduct FEA on seat structures for strength and safety compliance and work with our safety team on FMVSS testing requirements. Understanding of thermal analysis is relevant for heated and ventilated seat systems. This role offers a blend of creative design and hardcore engineering. Nashville's growing EV ecosystem and vibrant city life make this an attractive opportunity. We offer competitive pay, a downtown office location, and a team that genuinely enjoys working together.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering or Industrial Design Engineering
- 3-6 years of automotive interior design experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS; Fusion 360 experience preferred
- Strong GD&T knowledge for fit and finish specifications
- FEA experience for structural analysis
- Knowledge of DFM for injection molding and thermoforming
- Understanding of thermal analysis for climate-controlled seating
- Familiarity with FMVSS interior safety standards
- AutoCAD experience
- Creative problem-solving with attention to detail

title: Battery Thermal Engineer
description: CellCore Energy manufactures cylindrical and prismatic battery cells for the EV industry. We are looking for a Battery Thermal Engineer to optimize cell-level and module-level thermal performance. You will develop thermal models in ANSYS and validate them against calorimetry and thermocouple data from our test labs. CFD simulation of coolant channels and air gaps is a key part of the workflow. You will also build lumped-parameter thermal models in MATLAB/Simulink that feed into our Battery Management System algorithms for real-time thermal estimation. Understanding of thermal runaway propagation and mitigation strategies is essential for this safety-critical role. SOLIDWORKS is used for thermal interface material (TIM) selection studies and module packaging. GD&T knowledge ensures proper tolerancing of thermal contact surfaces. Python scripting is used for automating simulation sweeps and data post-processing. Our Fremont facility is adjacent to the factory floor, giving you direct access to production cells for testing. We are looking for someone who combines deep thermal analysis skills with practical manufacturing awareness.
requirements:
- MS in Mechanical Engineering (thermal/fluids focus)
- 3-5 years of battery thermal engineering experience
- Proficiency in ANSYS for thermal and CFD analysis
- Strong MATLAB/Simulink skills for lumped-parameter modeling
- Experience with SOLIDWORKS
- Understanding of GD&T for thermal interface tolerancing
- Knowledge of BMS thermal algorithms
- Python programming for simulation and data analysis
- Understanding of thermal runaway and battery safety
- FEA experience for thermo-mechanical stress analysis

title: Lean Manufacturing Engineer
description: GreenLine Assembly operates a high-volume EV component manufacturing facility in Chattanooga. We are looking for a Lean Manufacturing Engineer to drive continuous improvement across our production lines. You will lead kaizen events, implement 5S programs, conduct value stream mapping, and deploy Lean Six Sigma methodologies to reduce waste and improve throughput. This role is deeply embedded in the factory. You will spend 80% of your time on the shop floor observing processes, collecting data, and coaching production teams on standard work. AutoCAD and SOLIDWORKS are used for workstation and cell layout design. Understanding of GD&T is necessary for collaborating with quality engineers on process capability studies. SPC knowledge is important for monitoring critical process parameters. You will also support PFMEA activities and work within our IATF 16949 quality management framework. Experience with ERP systems, specifically SAP, is needed for tracking production metrics and material flow. We need someone with the interpersonal skills to influence operators and supervisors alongside the analytical chops to identify and eliminate root causes of inefficiency.
requirements:
- BS in Industrial, Mechanical, or Manufacturing Engineering
- 4-7 years of lean manufacturing experience in automotive
- Lean Six Sigma Green or Black Belt certification
- Proficiency in AutoCAD and SOLIDWORKS
- Knowledge of GD&T
- Experience with SPC and process capability analysis
- Familiarity with IATF 16949 quality standards
- Experience with ERP systems (SAP preferred)
- Strong lean manufacturing skills (kaizen, 5S, value stream mapping)
- Understanding of PFMEA and control plans
- Excellent communication and coaching abilities

title: Mechanical Engineering Intern - EV Battery
description: NovaBattery Corp is offering a 12-week summer internship for undergraduate Mechanical Engineering students interested in the EV battery industry. As an intern, you will support our battery pack design team by creating 3D models in SOLIDWORKS, assisting with GD&T on component drawings, and helping to analyze test data using MATLAB. You will get hands-on experience with battery module assembly in our prototype lab and learn about DFM considerations for high-volume battery production. This is an excellent opportunity to gain real-world engineering experience and build your network in the EV industry. Interns who are preparing for the FE exam will find this experience directly relevant to exam topics in mechanics, materials, and thermal systems. We assign each intern a mentor and a defined project with deliverables that you will present to our leadership team at the end of the summer. No prior EV experience is required, just a willingness to learn and a genuine passion for sustainable transportation. Competitive hourly rate and housing stipend provided.
requirements:
- Currently pursuing a BS in Mechanical Engineering (junior or senior standing)
- Coursework in mechanics of materials, thermodynamics, and machine design
- Basic SOLIDWORKS proficiency
- Understanding of GD&T fundamentals
- MATLAB exposure from coursework
- Interest in electric vehicles and energy storage
- Preparing for the FE exam is a plus
- Strong attention to detail and eagerness to learn
- Effective communication skills
- US work authorization required

title: Senior Vehicle Dynamics Engineer
description: AeroVolt Racing is applying Formula E technology to road-going EVs. We need a Senior Vehicle Dynamics Engineer to tune the ride, handling, and stability characteristics of our first production sports car. You will define suspension targets based on competitive benchmarking, develop vehicle models in MATLAB/Simulink, and work with the suspension hardware team to achieve target performance on our development track. This is a senior role where you set the vision for how the car should feel and drive. You will use data acquisition systems with CAN bus integration for on-track testing, process data in MATLAB and Python, and correlate with simulation predictions. Finite Element Analysis of suspension components supports your load case definitions. You will also interface with the regenerative braking calibration team to ensure regen torque delivery is smooth and predictable. Knowledge of structural analysis for suspension attachment points is expected. Our Irvine facility includes a vehicle dynamics simulator and rolling road. This is a rare opportunity to bridge motorsport and production EV engineering. Competitive salary, performance bonuses, and team trackday events included.
requirements:
- MS in Mechanical or Automotive Engineering
- 8+ years of vehicle dynamics experience
- Proficiency in MATLAB/Simulink for vehicle modeling
- Experience with CAN bus data acquisition systems
- Knowledge of SOLIDWORKS or CATIA
- FEA skills for structural analysis of suspension components
- Understanding of GD&T
- Python programming for data analysis
- Experience with regenerative braking system integration
- Understanding of Finite Element Analysis for load case development

title: Entry-Level Mechanical Engineer
description: PioneerEV Inc. is looking for an Entry-Level Mechanical Engineer to join our powertrain development team in Detroit. This is an excellent opportunity for recent graduates who want to launch their career in the electric vehicle industry. You will assist senior engineers with component design in SOLIDWORKS, learn to apply GD&T on production drawings, and support prototype assembly activities. We will train you on our simulation tools including ANSYS for FEA and MATLAB for data processing. You will be exposed to the full product development cycle from concept through production launch. This role includes a structured 12-month rotation program where you spend time in design, testing, manufacturing, and quality departments. We invest heavily in early-career talent development including sponsorship for the FE exam and Professional Engineering licensure. Our Detroit office is in the Corktown neighborhood, a revitalized area buzzing with EV startups and automotive innovation. Starting salary is competitive, and we offer student loan repayment assistance up to $5,000 per year.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering (recent graduate or up to 2 years experience)
- SOLIDWORKS coursework or project experience
- Basic understanding of GD&T
- Exposure to MATLAB or Python
- Interest in electric vehicles and powertrain systems
- FE exam passed or scheduled (EIT certification a plus)
- Strong academic record (GPA 3.0+)
- Hands-on project experience (FSAE, capstone, or similar)
- Good communication and teamwork skills
- US work authorization required

title: Electrical Harness Design Engineer
description: WireVolt Solutions specializes in electrical distribution systems for EVs. We are hiring an Electrical Harness Design Engineer to design wiring harnesses and connector systems for high-voltage and low-voltage vehicle networks. While this is an electro-mechanical role, it is firmly rooted in mechanical engineering. You will route harnesses through 3D vehicle models in CATIA, design brackets and clips in SOLIDWORKS, and ensure all routing meets bend radius and clearance requirements. GD&T knowledge is needed for connector interface drawings, and tolerance stack-up analysis ensures proper mating with vehicle-mounted connectors. Understanding of CAN bus network architecture helps you work effectively with the electrical systems team. You will participate in DFM reviews for harness manufacturing processes including terminal crimping, ultrasonic welding, and tape wrapping. DFMEA activities are part of every program launch. The Dearborn automotive ecosystem offers unmatched networking opportunities, and WireVolt is a growing company with strong OEM relationships. We offer flexible hours, professional development budget, and a collaborative open-office environment.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering
- 3-5 years of wiring harness design experience
- Proficiency in CATIA V5 and SOLIDWORKS
- Understanding of GD&T and tolerance stack-up analysis
- Knowledge of CAN bus and vehicle network architecture
- Experience with DFM for harness manufacturing
- DFMEA participation experience
- AutoCAD skills for schematic drawings
- Familiarity with BMS harness integration
- Strong organizational and documentation skills

title: Battery Module Design Engineer
description: ArcCell Technologies is a fast-growing battery technology company developing ultra-fast-charging modules for the EV market. We need a Battery Module Design Engineer to own the mechanical design of our module platform. You will design cell holders, busbars, compression systems, and thermal interface assemblies in SOLIDWORKS. FEA validation of your designs under thermal cycling, vibration, and abuse load cases is a core part of the workflow using ANSYS. You should be deeply familiar with GD&T for high-precision assemblies where cell-to-cell spacing tolerances are critical for both performance and safety. Tolerance stack-up analysis is performed on every new design revision. BMS integration knowledge is important since sensor routing and measurement point placement directly impact your mechanical package. Python scripts automate our simulation batch runs and data extraction. We leverage Fusion 360 for early-stage concept studies before moving to SOLIDWORKS for production intent. Our San Jose team is small, technical, and mission-driven. We offer competitive salary, equity participation, and a yearly professional development fund.
requirements:
- BS or MS in Mechanical Engineering
- 3-6 years of battery module or pack design experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS and Fusion 360
- FEA experience in ANSYS
- Strong GD&T and tolerance stack-up skills
- Knowledge of BMS and high-voltage safety
- Python scripting ability
- Understanding of thermal analysis for battery systems
- DFM/DFA knowledge for volume production
- Experience with MATLAB for data analysis

title: EV Platform Systems Engineer
description: NextGen Mobility is developing a modular EV platform architecture for multiple vehicle segments. We need a Systems Engineer to manage the interfaces and requirements across battery, powertrain, chassis, body, and thermal subsystems. This is a systems engineering role that requires strong mechanical fundamentals. You will use model-based systems engineering (MBSE) tools and SysML to capture requirements, define interfaces, and trace verification. MATLAB/Simulink is used for system-level performance modeling. You should be comfortable in SOLIDWORKS for reviewing component designs and packaging studies. Project management skills are essential since you will coordinate cross-functional teams and track deliverables across concurrent vehicle programs. You will lead system-level DFMEA activities and support vehicle-level validation planning. Understanding of BMS integration, electric powertrain architecture, and CAN bus communication is expected. AutoCAD skills are helpful for system architecture diagrams. We are building something big in Austin, and this role gives you the broad view of how an entire EV platform comes together. Competitive compensation, stock options, and flexible PTO.
requirements:
- BS or MS in Mechanical Engineering or Systems Engineering
- 5-8 years of automotive systems engineering experience
- Experience with MBSE tools and SysML
- Proficiency in MATLAB/Simulink
- SOLIDWORKS and AutoCAD skills
- Strong project management abilities; PMP or Agile experience preferred
- Knowledge of GD&T
- Understanding of BMS, electric powertrain, and CAN bus
- DFMEA leadership experience
- Strong technical writing and documentation skills
- Systems engineering mindset with attention to interface management

title: Mechanical Simulation Engineer
description: SimForce Engineering provides advanced simulation services to the automotive industry. We are hiring a Mechanical Simulation Engineer who lives and breathes FEA. You will perform linear and nonlinear structural analysis, modal analysis, thermal stress analysis, and explicit dynamics simulations for EV components. Our primary tools are ANSYS Mechanical, LS-DYNA, and Abaqus, and we expect strong proficiency in at least two of these. You will build simulation models from SOLIDWORKS and CATIA geometry, applying appropriate meshing strategies, material models, and boundary conditions. GD&T understanding helps you identify critical areas requiring detailed analysis. MATLAB and Python are used for pre/post-processing and scripting. This role requires deep understanding of structural analysis fundamentals including elasticity, plasticity, contact mechanics, and fracture mechanics. Fatigue analysis using stress-life and strain-life approaches is part of the workload. We also perform vibration analysis including random vibration and harmonic response. Our Detroit office serves both legacy OEMs and EV startups, giving you variety in every project. Senior simulation engineers at SimForce earn top-of-market compensation with a profit-sharing bonus structure.
requirements:
- MS or PhD in Mechanical Engineering (structural mechanics focus)
- 4-8 years of FEA simulation experience
- Expert proficiency in ANSYS and/or LS-DYNA
- Experience with SOLIDWORKS and CATIA for geometry preparation
- Strong GD&T knowledge
- MATLAB and Python scripting skills
- Deep understanding of structural analysis principles
- Experience with fatigue analysis methods
- Knowledge of vibration analysis (modal, harmonic, random)
- Thermal analysis capability for thermo-mechanical problems
- Excellent technical writing for simulation reports

title: EV Charging Infrastructure Engineer
description: PowerGrid Innovations designs and deploys EV charging solutions for commercial real estate and fleet operators. We need a Mechanical Engineer focused on charging infrastructure to design mounting systems, cable management solutions, and equipment enclosures for Level 2 and DC fast chargers. You will use Fusion 360 and AutoCAD for site-specific installation designs and SOLIDWORKS for product development. This role involves a mix of product design and field engineering. You will visit installation sites, assess structural conditions, and create custom mounting details. Understanding of DFM for sheet metal and welded fabrications is important. GD&T skills are needed for hardware interface drawings. FEA is used to validate structural adequacy of mounting systems under wind, seismic, and vehicle impact loads. The Phoenix market is booming for EV infrastructure, and this role offers the chance to directly shape the charging landscape in the Southwest. We offer competitive pay, a company vehicle, and a results-oriented work culture. If you enjoy both CAD work and field problem-solving, this hybrid role is ideal.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical or Civil Engineering
- 2-5 years of product design or structural engineering experience
- Proficiency in Fusion 360 and AutoCAD
- SOLIDWORKS experience
- Understanding of GD&T
- FEA skills for structural load analysis
- Knowledge of DFM for sheet metal fabrication
- Ability to read and create construction drawings
- Strong technical writing for installation documentation
- Willingness to travel to job sites (25-30%)

title: Powertrain Calibration Mechanical Engineer
description: DrivePulse Technologies is seeking a Mechanical Engineer specializing in powertrain calibration support. You will design and fabricate test fixtures, instrumentation mounts, and sensor brackets for our engine and motor dynamometer test cells. While the calibration engineers tune the software, you keep the hardware running smoothly. SOLIDWORKS is your primary design tool, and you will create fixtures that are robust enough for continuous dyno operation while allowing quick reconfiguration between test articles. Knowledge of CNC machining helps you spec parts that can be turned around quickly by our in-house machine shop. Understanding of thermal analysis is relevant because sensor placement must account for thermal gradients in exhaust and motor cooling systems. GD&T is applied to ensure repeatable fixturing. You will also design custom adapters for CAN bus breakout boxes and data acquisition hardware. This is a hands-on role in a lab environment. We use MATLAB for test data analysis and you will generate reports summarizing mechanical test observations. Familiarity with electric powertrain components rounds out the requirements. Dearborn is the historic capital of automotive engineering, and DrivePulse is proud to continue that legacy in the EV era.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering
- 2-4 years of test lab or prototype engineering experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS
- Understanding of GD&T
- Knowledge of CNC machining processes
- MATLAB skills for data analysis
- Familiarity with thermal analysis for sensor placement
- CAN bus hardware familiarity
- Experience with electric powertrain testing
- Hands-on mechanical skills

title: Senior Structural Analysis Engineer
description: TitanFrame EV develops lightweight structural solutions for electric vehicle platforms. We are hiring a Senior Structural Analysis Engineer to lead the structural design and analysis of battery tray assemblies, motor mounts, and subframe components. You will perform advanced FEA including nonlinear contact analysis, bolted joint modeling, and weld fatigue assessment using ANSYS and HyperMesh. SOLIDWORKS proficiency is required for design modifications and geometric cleanup prior to meshing. Strong understanding of structural analysis fundamentals is non-negotiable, including stress-strain relationships, failure theories, and buckling. You will apply GD&T to define critical dimensions based on stress analysis results and manufacturing constraints. DFM/DFA considerations for aluminum casting and stamping are integral to your design recommendations. Tolerance stack-up analysis ensures structural joints maintain preload under all conditions. We also perform fatigue analysis for durability sign-off and vibration analysis for resonance avoidance. This is a senior role where you will mentor junior analysts and set simulation standards. CATIA skills are a bonus for OEM data exchange. We value deep technical competence and offer compensation to match.
requirements:
- MS in Mechanical Engineering (structural mechanics)
- 8+ years of structural analysis experience in automotive
- Expert FEA skills in ANSYS, HyperMesh, or Abaqus
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS; CATIA experience a plus
- Strong GD&T knowledge
- Experience with structural analysis including nonlinear and contact
- Fatigue analysis and vibration analysis experience
- Tolerance stack-up analysis skills
- Understanding of DFM/DFA for cast and stamped components
- MATLAB or Python for post-processing
- Mentorship and technical leadership experience

title: EV Motor Test Engineer
description: ElectraSpin Motors designs high-efficiency electric motors for passenger and commercial EVs. We are hiring a Motor Test Engineer to plan and execute performance, endurance, and environmental testing of our motor assemblies. You will operate and maintain dynamometer test cells, develop test automation scripts in Python, and process results using MATLAB. This role requires mechanical aptitude for motor assembly and disassembly, instrumentation installation, and troubleshooting test anomalies. SOLIDWORKS is used for test fixture design, and you should apply GD&T for repeatable fixturing. Vibration analysis skills help you diagnose bearing, eccentricity, and electromagnetic noise issues. Understanding of FEA is beneficial for correlating test failures with predicted stress hotspots. You will document test procedures and results thoroughly, making technical writing an important skill. We are expanding our test capabilities in Greenville and need someone who can grow with us. Our facility is modern, well-equipped, and backed by significant investment in next-generation motor technology. Competitive salary with relocation support available.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering
- 2-5 years of motor or powertrain testing experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS
- Python and MATLAB skills for test automation and data analysis
- Understanding of GD&T for fixture design
- Experience with vibration analysis
- Familiarity with FEA for test-analysis correlation
- Knowledge of electric motor construction and operation
- Strong technical writing skills
- Hands-on mechanical skills for motor assembly/disassembly

title: Regenerative Braking Systems Engineer
description: BrakeGen Automotive is a specialist supplier of regenerative braking systems for EVs. We are hiring a Systems Engineer to design and integrate regen braking hardware for OEM vehicle platforms. You will work on electromechanical actuators, brake-by-wire components, and energy recovery optimization. SOLIDWORKS is used for component design and CATIA for OEM interface models. MATLAB/Simulink is critical for modeling regenerative braking torque distribution, blending regen and friction braking, and evaluating energy recovery under various drive cycles. Understanding of BMS integration is needed since regen power flows directly to the battery, and you must coordinate with battery limits. FEA is performed on caliper brackets and actuator housings for structural validation. GD&T is applied to all safety-critical brake interfaces with tight tolerances. Knowledge of CAN bus communication is essential for brake system controls integration. You will also conduct DFMEA for all brake system components per IATF 16949 requirements. This is a specialized role at the intersection of mechanical design, controls, and vehicle dynamics. If you are passionate about regenerative braking technology and want to work with a leader in the field, apply today.
requirements:
- BS or MS in Mechanical Engineering
- 4-7 years of brake system or regenerative braking experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS and CATIA
- Strong MATLAB/Simulink skills for system modeling
- FEA experience for structural analysis
- Thorough GD&T knowledge
- Understanding of BMS and battery charge acceptance
- CAN bus communication experience
- DFMEA experience per IATF 16949
- Knowledge of electric powertrain energy management
- Experience with regenerative braking calibration and testing

title: Product Design Engineer - EV Accessories
description: ChargeMate Products designs and sells consumer-facing EV accessories including portable chargers, cable organizers, and vehicle-mounted storage solutions. We are looking for a Product Design Engineer to take products from concept through production. This is a consumer products role within the EV ecosystem, and we want someone who sweats the details on features like cable retention mechanisms, magnetic closures, and user-friendly interfaces. You will model products in Fusion 360 and SOLIDWORKS, creating designs optimized for injection molding and overmolding processes. Understanding of DFM for injection molding is essential, including draft angles, wall thickness, and gate placement. GD&T is used for assembly interfaces and vendor drawing packages. AutoCAD supports packaging and installation instruction drawings. We are a small team that ships products through direct-to-consumer and retail channels, so you will see your work on shelves within months of design completion. No Finite Element Analysis deep-dive needed for this role, but basic stress checks in SolidWorks Simulation are expected. Irvine location with hybrid schedule. Great for someone who loves consumer product design and the EV space.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical Engineering or Product Design
- 2-5 years of consumer product design experience
- Proficiency in Fusion 360 and SOLIDWORKS
- Knowledge of DFM for injection molding
- Understanding of GD&T
- AutoCAD skills
- Experience taking products from concept to mass production
- Familiarity with prototyping methods including 3D printing
- Strong aesthetic sense and attention to user experience features
- Portfolio demonstrating consumer product design work

title: Controls Integration Mechanical Engineer
description: AutoPilot EV Systems develops advanced driver assistance and autonomous driving hardware for electric vehicles. We are seeking a Mechanical Engineer specializing in sensor packaging and controls hardware integration. You will design mounting brackets, enclosures, and thermal solutions for cameras, LiDAR units, radar modules, and compute platforms. Packaging these components within the vehicle body while maintaining aesthetic requirements and serviceability demands creative problem-solving. SOLIDWORKS is our primary CAD tool, with CATIA used for vehicle-level integration. FEA validates bracket strength under vibration and thermal cycling loads. GD&T ensures precise sensor alignment critical for perception accuracy. You will perform tolerance stack-up analysis to guarantee pointing accuracy is maintained across production variation. Thermal analysis of compute modules is a regular task since these components generate significant heat. Python scripting helps automate design checks and data extraction. Understanding of CAN bus architecture aids communication with the software team on sensor trigger timing and data flow. This is a multidisciplinary role at the intersection of mechanical, electrical, and software engineering. Palo Alto compensation includes base, bonus, and equity.
requirements:
- BS or MS in Mechanical Engineering
- 3-6 years of experience in sensor packaging or electronics integration
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS and CATIA
- FEA skills for structural and thermal analysis
- Strong GD&T and tolerance stack-up expertise
- Python scripting ability
- Understanding of thermal analysis for electronics cooling
- Familiarity with CAN bus and vehicle networks
- DFM knowledge for die cast and machined housings
- AutoCAD for installation documentation

title: EV Drivetrain NVH Engineer
description: SilentDrive Engineering specializes in noise, vibration, and harshness solutions for EV drivetrains. Our Ann Arbor team needs an NVH Engineer focused specifically on gear whine, bearing noise, and electromagnetic force excitation in electric drive units. You will use a combination of analytical models in MATLAB, FEA modal analysis in ANSYS, and experimental testing to diagnose and mitigate NVH issues. Vibration analysis of rotating assemblies is the core competency we require. You will design test fixtures and measurement setups in SOLIDWORKS, applying GD&T to ensure measurement repeatability. Python is used for order tracking analysis and data visualization. Experience with C++ is a plus for integrating with our proprietary NVH analysis software platform. Understanding of structural analysis for housing stiffness optimization helps you design quieter motor enclosures. This role involves frequent collaboration with motor designers, gear engineers, and vehicle integration teams at our OEM customers. Ann Arbor is a university town with a vibrant culture, excellent restaurants, and easy access to Detroit OEM engineering centers. Competitive salary, 401(k) match, and annual professional development budget.
requirements:
- MS in Mechanical Engineering (dynamics, acoustics, or vibration)
- 4-7 years of NVH engineering experience in automotive
- Proficiency in ANSYS for modal and harmonic analysis
- Strong MATLAB skills for signal processing and data analysis
- Experience with vibration analysis of rotating machinery
- SOLIDWORKS proficiency for fixture design
- GD&T knowledge
- Python programming skills
- C++ experience is a plus
- Understanding of structural analysis for housing design
- FEA skills for modal and frequency response analysis

title: Manufacturing Quality Technician to Engineer Pipeline
description: VoltDrive Motors is offering a unique pathway for experienced Quality Technicians looking to transition into an engineering role. Based at our new Chattanooga assembly plant, you will start in a hands-on quality inspection role and participate in a concurrent engineering education program we sponsor. Day-to-day you will use GD&T knowledge to interpret blueprints, perform dimensional inspection using CMMs and hand gauges, conduct SPC analysis on critical dimensions, and support root cause analysis for production quality issues using 8D methodology. As you progress, you will take on responsibilities including PFMEA updates, control plan revisions, and PPAP documentation. SOLIDWORKS skills will be developed through our internal training program. We operate under IATF 16949 and ISO 9001, so familiarity with quality management system auditing is expected. Understanding of lean manufacturing principles including 5S and visual management supports our daily operations. This program has placed 12 technicians into engineering roles over the past three years. If you have a technical associate's degree and hunger to grow, VoltDrive will invest in your development.

- Associate's degree in Mechanical or Manufacturing Technology (BS preferred)
- 3+ years of quality inspection experience in automotive
- Strong GD&T reading and interpretation ability
- Knowledge of SPC and measurement systems analysis
- Experience with root cause analysis (8D)
- Familiarity with IATF 16949 and ISO 9001
- Understanding of APQP and PPAP
- Basic SOLIDWORKS skills (training provided)
- Lean manufacturing awareness (5S, visual management)
- Passion for continuous learning and career growth

title: Thermal Simulation Engineer
description: HeatWise Engineering provides thermal simulation consulting to EV and tech companies in Silicon Valley. We are hiring a Thermal Simulation Engineer to perform CFD and conjugate heat transfer analyses for battery packs, power electronics, and motor cooling systems. ANSYS Fluent and ANSYS Mechanical are our primary tools, and you will build detailed simulation models from CAD geometry provided in SOLIDWORKS, CATIA, and Creo formats. GD&T understanding helps you interpret drawing tolerances that affect thermal contact resistance and gap conductance assumptions in your models. MATLAB and Python are used for automating parametric sweeps and generating performance maps. Thermal analysis is obviously the core of this role, but we also perform thermo-mechanical FEA to evaluate thermal stress and warpage in electronic assemblies. Understanding of DFM helps you provide actionable recommendations that clients can actually implement. Our clients range from Fortune 500 OEMs to Series A startups, so you will see incredible variety. San Jose office with flexible hours and remote Fridays. We value engineers who can communicate complex simulation results in clear, practical terms.
requirements:
- MS in Mechanical Engineering (thermal/fluids focus)
- 3-6 years of thermal simulation experience
- Expert proficiency in ANSYS Fluent and ANSYS Mechanical
- Experience with SOLIDWORKS, CATIA, and Creo for geometry handling
- Strong MATLAB and Python skills
- Deep thermal analysis expertise
- FEA knowledge for thermo-structural analysis
- Understanding of GD&T
- DFM awareness for practical design recommendations
- Strong technical writing and client-facing communication skills

title: Electric Vehicle Test Driver/Engineer
description: ProveOut Testing operates a desert proving ground near Phoenix where we validate EVs under extreme heat conditions. We are hiring a Test Driver/Engineer to support vehicle-level testing including range validation, thermal soak testing, charging performance, and durability driving. This is not a pure desk role. You will drive prototype vehicles on our test tracks and public roads, record observations, and instrument vehicles with sensors. Engineering tasks include designing test fixtures in SOLIDWORKS, analyzing data in MATLAB, and writing detailed test reports. GD&T knowledge helps when inspecting components after durability runs. Understanding of BMS behavior under thermal stress is important for interpreting charging and battery data. You will monitor CAN bus signals in real-time during test drives to flag anomalies. Familiarity with electric powertrain systems helps you communicate effectively with development engineers when reporting issues. This is an outdoors-heavy role in Arizona's extreme climate. We provide climate-controlled garages and strict heat safety protocols. If you love driving, testing, and the desert sun, ProveOut is the place. Competitive pay plus hazard differential for extreme weather testing.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical or Automotive Engineering
- 2-5 years of vehicle testing experience
- Valid driver's license with clean record
- SOLIDWORKS skills for fixture design
- MATLAB proficiency for data analysis
- Understanding of GD&T
- Knowledge of BMS and battery thermal behavior
- CAN bus data monitoring experience
- Familiarity with electric powertrain systems
- Strong technical writing for test documentation
- Physical fitness for demanding driving schedules in extreme conditions

title: Injection Molding Design Engineer
description: PlastiVolt Components supplies injection-molded plastic parts to the EV industry, including battery module housings, connector covers, HVAC ducting, and interior trim components. We need an Injection Molding Design Engineer at our Louisville facility. You will design parts in SOLIDWORKS optimized for the injection molding process, accounting for draft angles, uniform wall thickness, rib design, and knit line management. Moldflow simulation experience is a major plus. GD&T on your drawings must reflect the achievable tolerances for injection molded parts, and you will educate customers when their tolerances are unrealistic for the process. DFM is your bread and butter. You will conduct DFM reviews on every new part, providing feedback to OEM customers on design changes that reduce cost and improve quality. FEA is used for structural validation of housing and enclosure parts. Understanding of IATF 16949 is necessary as we supply safety-critical components. Tolerance stack-up analysis ensures your parts fit into multi-part assemblies. AutoCAD is used for plant-level tooling layouts. Louisville is an affordable and liveable city with a strong manufacturing base.
requirements:
- BS in Mechanical or Plastics Engineering
- 4-7 years of injection molding design experience
- Proficiency in SOLIDWORKS
- Expert-level DFM knowledge for injection molding
- Strong GD&T skills
- Tolerance stack-up analysis experience
